content:
for context on the mid depression, idk how much of your road was paved with aderall, if just the last leg of the tour or also the first leg…that is definitley a major contributing factor.

when i was on con-amtrak, i swallowed all the pills i acquired [found at the train station, officer], didn’t make a dent (un)fortunately, and was in withdrawal from what was a year long bender that evening, pulled into Newark 5 years later, met my potential soon-to-be neighbors in the station atrium, couldn’t halt the body’s entrance or exits from purging wholesale the content contained in the tunnels and caverns they shared betwixt, and haven’t put any poison into either entrance nor exit since.

my sleep is still properly fucked up, my REM remembers my avoidance of it, and is beating the shit out of me currently. can’t sleep for more than an 1-2 hours, been leaning into it however, if i get up at 3am, put on a movie, or do a little work until tired.

all this to say, hey, rest a bunch, treat yourself to lotsa good meals, in a week or two, you’ll start feeling better.

and finally arriving at what i really really wanna say, which is, thank you, for many things, but most importantly for being a great friend, more family-than-family in my estimation of things, and a lot of what you said in your speech about Daniel reflects clearly to me, and I hope you can see that about yourself as well.
context: From document: for context on the mid depression, idk how much of your road was paved… — chunk 1 (chunk 1/2)
